active_families.thesis: Edge improves when trend/transitional participation is retained and reward tails are extended carefully; focus is expectancy lift without losing density.
Funding-crowding snapback has tradable density; objective is risk-shape optimization to convert marginal PF into durable score improvements.
Transitional regime edge exists but trade count is too low; relax ADX band one notch to improve density while preserving regime intent.
Asset-transfer probe: test whether channel short-bias structure that improved on AXS can survive on ETH with better liquidity and lower execution friction.

abandoned_this_cycle.reason: Three-plus iterations with persistent one-trade outcomes; PF is non-inferential and not scalable.
Three-plus iterations retained one-trade pathology despite parameter changes.
v1 and v2 both produced zero trades after density unlock attempt; branch appears structurally inactive on ETH 1h.
Multiple high-density iterations remained sub-1 PF after costs; no durable regime-isolated rescue.
High-density but strongly negative expectancy (PF 0.60, deep drawdown), indicating thesis-sign failure.
Adequate trades but severe anti-edge (PF 0.41) and weak regime breakdown; no evidence of recoverable mechanism.
Defensive and balanced variants both produced zero trades, indicating structural entry failure.
Low density and anti-edge metrics provide no viable path to recovery.

new_this_cycle.thesis: Transport AXS short-bias channel architecture to ETH to test whether the mechanism is asset-agnostic.
